pattern: The count equals the unsigned Stirling number of the first kind, built by deciding whether the shortest stick starts a new visible run or hides behind any of the existing ones.
hints: Think about placing the longest stick: it is always visible, so the question is where the rest of the visibility comes from.
Consider adding the n-th stick to an arrangement of n-1: it can be the visible leftmost (adding a visible stick) or slip into any of the n-1 non-leftmost slots (keeping the visible count).
This gives dp[n][k] = dp[n-1][k-1] + (n-1)*dp[n-1][k] modulo 1e9+7, the unsigned Stirling numbers of the first kind.

edge_inputs.description: all sticks visible only one way
one visible means longest must be first

stress.description: random n with k in range, including the upper bound
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 1000)
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [n, k]
